ReleaseLabel: Extension pre-0.37.0 (Set 081 'Budget Input Scoped To The Direct-API Sub-Choice', Session 2 of 2)
Notes: Ad-hoc per-set UAT for Set 081, a change you yourself requested during the Set 080 walk: the Full tier's 'Verification budget (USD, not-to-exceed)' input applies only to the 'Direct provider API keys' sub-option, so it now renders as an indented child of that option row and is shown ONLY while that sub-option is selected -- and Build is honest about it: a Copilot-seat Build writes no ai_router/budget.yaml at all (absence has documented compat defaults). NO budget wording changed (label, help text, placeholder, $0 copy are all byte-identical to 0.36.0), NO budget.yaml schema change, and the Lightweight tier is untouched (it never showed the block). Authored to the Set 078/079/080 bar: every quoted string was re-grounded against CURRENT source during authoring (gettingStartedHtml.js BUDGET_* constants at lines 64-70, budgetBlockHtml at 356-386, transportProfileBlockHtml at 483-513; tree.css .gs-option-child at 485-491; budgetYaml.ts renderBudgetYaml at 104-113; gitScaffold.ts budgetNote at 522-527), and every cited Layer-2 suite was re-run fresh during authoring with the exact pass count quoted per walk (full suite fresh: 1265 passing / 0 failing; npx tsc --noEmit clean). UAT runs against a LOCALLY BUILT VSIX ONLY (the Set 058 lesson) -- the orchestrator built one for you during authoring at tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.36.0.vsix (it contains the Set 081 changes; the version bump to 0.37.0 happens at release, after this walk). Install it via 'Extensions: Install from VSIX...' -- or rebuild it yourself: in a terminal at tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration run `npx vsce package`. Walks 1-3 are webview walks that cannot be driven headless from this environment; the conditional markup, exact copy, omit-vs-hidden choice, and persistence precedence are fully pinned at Layer 2 (suites quoted per walk), so the operator-only portions there are the real rendered webview (indentation and separators painting correctly in your theme), the real setState/getState round-trip across an actual window reload, and one JUDGMENT item (whether the nesting reads clearly as 'this budget belongs to the Direct-API option' -- the entire point of this set). Walks 4-5 are the REAL Build dogfoods the set's spec names cold-start (L-079-3): one Build per sub-option, each from a genuinely FRESH EMPTY folder (no pre-seeded config), asserting the budget.yaml write matrix -- these are exactly what the Layer-2 seams cannot prove, because the stub never runs the real venv/pip/template subprocesses. Each Build takes a few minutes (venv + pip install). After the walk, this set also needs you to capture the refreshed getting-started.png README screenshot -- instructions are in the chat stop message, not in this checklist (it is not a pass/fail verification item).

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 -- budget block nested under the Direct-API row (Full + api)
Review.ItemLabel: With Full + 'Direct provider API keys' selected, the budget block renders as an indented child between the two option rows, wording unchanged from 0.36.0
Review.HumanAction: Install the locally built VSIX (see Notes). Open any folder in VS Code (a fresh empty folder is fine). Click the Dabbler AI Orchestration icon in the Activity Bar and look at the Getting Started form's step 1. The radio labeled exactly "Full" is checked by default, and inside the group labeled exactly "Provider access (how routed calls run)" the row "Direct provider API keys" is pre-checked. Look at what sits directly UNDER that first option row.
Review.Expectation:
Directly under the "Direct provider API keys" row -- and visibly indented so it clears the radio-button column and reads as belonging to that row -- sits the budget block:
- label exactly: "Verification budget (USD, not-to-exceed)"
- a text input with placeholder "25"
- help text exactly: "Spending cap for cross-provider verification, written to ai_router/budget.yaml. Enter 0 to opt out of paid verification."
Below the indented block, a single light horizontal line separates it from the second option row ("GitHub Copilot CLI seat"). The budget block appears NOWHERE else on the form -- in 0.36.0 it sat below the whole provider-access group; it has moved inside it. Typing 0 in the input still reveals the $0 rule copy exactly: "A $0 budget still needs a verification rule. Choose whether to check each session in another engine or skip verification." with the two radios "Check in another engine" and "Skip verification" (clear the 0 afterwards). All wording is character-for-character what 0.36.0 showed -- only the position and the conditionality changed.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "Set 081 S1"` fresh during authoring: 12/12 passing, including 'Full+api: budget block nests inside the transport block, under the Direct-API row' (asserts [data-gs-budget] sits inside [data-gs-transport-profile], wrapped in <div class="gs-option-child" data-gs-option-child="api"> between the api and copilot-cli rows) and 'legacy callers without a transportProfile field still render the block'. All quoted copy is verbatim from gettingStartedHtml.js:64-70 (BUDGET_LABEL_TEXT / BUDGET_HELP_TEXT / BUDGET_ZERO_CHOICE_TEXT) and the placeholder from budgetBlockHtml (line 370), re-read moments before this checklist was written. The indent (margin-left: 22px) and the separator-restore rule (.gs-option-child + .gs-option-row { border-top: 1px solid var(--vscode-panel-border) }) are tree.css:485-491. The real rendered webview is the operator portion.

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 -- budget block nested under the Direct-API row (Full + api)
Review.ItemLabel: JUDGMENT: the nesting reads at a glance as 'this budget applies to the Direct-API option'
Review.HumanAction: Look at the same group and judge it against your own Set 080 request: the budget label + input should sit below the "Direct provider API keys" option and read as scoped to it, not as a form-wide setting.
Review.Expectation: At a glance -- without reading the help text -- the budget input reads as a child of the "Direct provider API keys" option rather than as a setting that applies to the whole group. Pass only if YOU find the scoping obvious; this row exists precisely for your judgment, not for a mechanical check.
Review.JudgmentJustification: Whether the indentation 'reads as belonging to the option above' is an aesthetic legibility call only the requesting operator can attest; the mechanical nesting behind it is covered by the functional item above.

Review.Subarea: Walk 2 -- no budget block on Copilot seat or Lightweight
Review.ItemLabel: Selecting 'GitHub Copilot CLI seat' removes the budget block entirely; Lightweight never shows it
Review.HumanAction:
1. In the "Provider access (how routed calls run)" group, click the row "GitHub Copilot CLI seat" (anywhere in the row). (A warning about a missing Copilot CLI may appear if the copilot command is not on this machine's PATH -- that is pre-existing Set 079 behavior; ignore it either way.)
2. Look at the whole form for any budget label or input.
3. Click the radio labeled exactly "Lightweight" and look again.
4. Click "Full" again -- your step-1 pick is preserved, so "GitHub Copilot CLI seat" is still selected. Cleanup: click the "Direct provider API keys" row to end back on the default (its budget block reappears -- that is Walk 1 behavior).
Review.Expectation: With "GitHub Copilot CLI seat" selected: the label "Verification budget (USD, not-to-exceed)" and its input appear NOWHERE on the form -- the block is gone entirely (not grayed out, not collapsed), and the two provider-access option rows sit directly adjacent with a single light line between them, exactly like the Verification group looks on Lightweight. With "Lightweight" selected: no budget block anywhere either (unchanged from 0.36.0 -- Lightweight never had one), and no "Provider access" group at all. Build is NOT blocked by any hidden budget: nothing anywhere asks for a budget while the Copilot seat option is selected.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Same fresh `npm run test:unit -- --grep "Set 081 S1"` run (12/12): 'Full+copilot: budget block absent entirely (omitted, not hidden)' asserts neither [data-gs-budget] nor the .gs-option-child wrapper renders (so the adjacent-row separator rule applies directly), and 'Lightweight: absent regardless of the sub-choice value' pins the Lightweight arm. The never-trips-Build-validation half is pinned by the fresh `npm run test:unit -- --grep "build matrix"` run (7/7): 'Full+copilot WITHOUT budget riders dispatches (no fail-closed reject)' -- the Set 063 fail-closed rejection is now scoped to Full+api (client.js:344-358 validates the budget only while transportProfile !== "copilot-cli"). The real rendered webview is the operator portion.

Review.Subarea: Walk 3 -- typed budget survives sub-choice flips and a window reload
Review.ItemLabel: A typed budget value survives an api -> copilot -> api flip round-trip and a full window reload; hiding never clears it
Review.HumanAction:
1. With "Full" and "Direct provider API keys" selected, click into the budget input and type exactly: 12
2. Click the "GitHub Copilot CLI seat" row. The budget block disappears (Walk 2).
3. Click the "Direct provider API keys" row again.
4. Confirm the budget input shows 12 (not empty, not the placeholder).
5. Run 'Developer: Reload Window' from the command palette and re-open the Getting Started form.
6. Confirm "Full" + "Direct provider API keys" are selected and the budget input still shows 12. Cleanup: clear the input if you plan to reuse this folder.
Review.Expectation: The typed value 12 is restored exactly after the flip round-trip (step 4) AND after the full window reload (step 6). Flipping to the Copilot option hides the block but never clears the typed value -- nothing snaps back to empty or to the placeholder "25".
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Flip-round-trip persistence is pinned in the fresh 12/12 'Set 081 S1' run: 'persistence: typed value survives an api -> copilot -> api flip' and 'persistence: the $0 zero-rule pick survives the flip round-trip too' (gsState carries budget/zeroMethod across the sub-choice re-render; the block is omitted, never cleared -- gettingStartedHtml.js:342-355 comment documents the omit choice). Reload-restore precedence is pinned by the fresh `npm run test:unit -- --grep "restoreGsState"` run: 26/26 passing (Set 077 S2 base + S3 mode-seed + Set 079 S1 profile-seed suites, all UNCHANGED by this set -- Session 1's scope tripwire held). The real vscode setState/getState round-trip across an actual window reload is inherently untestable on the vscode-stub and is the operator portion.

Review.Subarea: Walk 4 -- REAL Build, Direct-API path, fresh empty folder (cold start)
Review.ItemLabel: A Full + Direct-API Build with budget 25 writes ai_router/budget.yaml with the api method
Review.HumanAction:
1. Create a brand-new EMPTY folder (example: C:\temp\uat-081-api) -- do not copy anything into it; the cold start is the point.
2. Open that folder in VS Code (File > Open Folder...) and open the Dabbler Getting Started form.
3. Leave "Full" and "Direct provider API keys" selected. Type exactly 25 in the budget input.
4. Click the button labeled exactly "Build project structure" and wait for the progress notification "Building project structure..." to finish (a few minutes: it creates a venv and pip-installs the router).
5. Read the completion notification.
6. In the folder, open the file ai_router/budget.yaml.
Review.Expectation:
The completion notification starts "Project structure built (full tier): ..." and ends with exactly: "Budget saved to ai_router/budget.yaml." The file ai_router/budget.yaml EXISTS and contains these lines (plus a set_at timestamp line):
threshold_usd: 25
scope: per-project
mode: "middle-tier"
verification_method: "api"
verification_nte_usd: 25
set_by: "getting-started-form"
This is the unchanged Set 063 behavior on the api path -- the set must NOT have broken it.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: The caller condition is pinned at the runScaffold seam by the fresh `npm run test:unit -- --grep "build matrix"` run (7/7): 'build matrix: api+budget passes the budget through to the scaffold step' asserts the scaffold step receives { thresholdUsd: 25 } under the api profile (gitScaffold.ts:461-462 effectiveBudget). The write itself is pinned by the fresh `npm run test:unit -- --grep "writeBudgetYaml"` run (3/3): 'writes ai_router/budget.yaml when absent' (budgetYaml.ts renderBudgetYaml:104-113 is the source of the expected lines; 25 falls in the 20-99 'middle-tier' mode band). What Layer 2 CANNOT prove is the real end-to-end subprocess path from a genuinely empty folder (L-079-3, the Set 079 lesson: a pre-seeded fixture masks first-run defects) -- that is exactly this operator walk.

Review.Subarea: Walk 5 -- REAL Build, Copilot-seat path, fresh empty folder (cold start)
Review.ItemLabel: A Full + Copilot-seat Build never asks for a budget and writes NO ai_router/budget.yaml
Review.HumanAction:
1. Create a second brand-new EMPTY folder (example: C:\temp\uat-081-copilot).
2. Open it in VS Code and open the Dabbler Getting Started form.
3. Leave "Full" selected and click the "GitHub Copilot CLI seat" row. Confirm no budget input is shown anywhere (Walk 2) -- you will type no budget in this walk.
4. Click "Build project structure" and wait for it to finish. (Copilot-seat-setup messages -- including a skip warning if the copilot CLI is not installed on this machine -- are pre-existing Set 079 behavior; note them if odd, but they are not this walk's verdict.)
5. Read the completion notification.
6. In the folder, look inside the ai_router directory for a file named budget.yaml.
Review.Expectation: Build ran WITHOUT ever asking for a budget -- no validation error, no budget prompt. The completion notification says "Project structure built (full tier): ..." and does NOT contain the sentence "Budget saved to ai_router/budget.yaml." (and not "Existing ai_router/budget.yaml kept" either -- no budget sentence at all). The file ai_router/budget.yaml does NOT exist -- the ai_router directory has other scaffolded files (e.g. router-config.yaml) but no budget.yaml. A missing budget.yaml is a documented, supported state (compat defaults in docs/budget-yaml-schema.md); the operator authors one later if the project ever flips back to the api profile.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: The drop is pinned at BOTH defense lines by the fresh `npm run test:unit -- --grep "build matrix"` run (7/7): action layer -- 'Full+copilot WITH budget riders dispatches with the budget dropped' (gettingStartedActions.ts asBudgetChoice returns undefined under copilot-cli); caller layer -- 'build matrix: copilot-cli drops the budget even when a caller passes one' and 'build matrix: copilot-cli with no budget passes undefined (the normal seat path)' at the runScaffold seam. With no budget, writeBudgetYaml is never invoked (scaffoldConsumerRepo gates on deps.budget; the no-write arm's outcome is null so gitScaffold.ts:522-527 emits no budget sentence). What Layer 2 CANNOT prove is the real cold-start absence on disk after the true subprocess Build (L-079-3) -- this walk.

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 -- budget block nested under the Direct-API row (Full + api)
Walk 2 -- no budget block on Copilot seat or Lightweight
Walk 3 -- typed budget survives sub-choice flips and a window reload
Walk 4 -- REAL Build, Direct-API path, fresh empty folder (cold start)
Review.Feedback: There should probably be one extra horizontal divider line between the second suboption the "Build project structure" button -- otherwise, it looks as though the button belongs to the second suboption. [REMEDIATED IN-SESSION 2026-07-05: the orchestrator added one tree.css rule closing BOTH sub-choice groups (Full's provider access, Lightweight's verification mode) with the same light border-bottom rule the rows use, plus a 6px gap before the button; gates re-run green (Layer-2 1265/0, tsc clean, Layer-3 green with one unrelated flake passing 4/4 in isolation); the operator reinstalled the rebuilt VSIX, re-checked the divider, and confirmed 'Done' via chat. Passes flipped by the orchestrator with this note, per the Set 080 chat-attestation precedent.]

Review.Subarea: Walk 5 -- REAL Build, Copilot-seat path, fresh empty folder (cold start)
